root_text.sn45.141:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kūṭāgārassa yā kāci gopānasiyo sabbā tā kūṭaṅgamā kūṭaninnā kūṭasamosaraṇā; kūṭaṁ tāsaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn45.142:2.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci mūlagandhā, kāḷānusāriyaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn45.143:3.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci sāragandhā, lohitacandanaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn45.144:4.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci pupphagandhā, vassikaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn45.145:5.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci kuṭṭarājāno, sabbe te rañño cakkavattissa anuyantā bhavanti, rājā tesaṁ cakkavatti aggamakkhāyati;

translation_text.sn45.141:1.1: “Mendicants, the rafters of a bungalow all lean to the peak, slope to the peak, and meet at the peak, so the peak is said to be the topmost of them all.
translation_text.sn45.141:1.3: (Tell in full as in the previous discourse.)
translation_text.sn45.142:2.1: “Of all kinds of fragrant root, spikenard is said to be the best. …”
translation_text.sn45.143:3.1: “Of all kinds of fragrant heartwood, red sandalwood is said to be the best. …”
translation_text.sn45.144:4.1: “Of all kinds of fragrant flower, jasmine is said to be the best. …”
translation_text.sn45.145:5.1: “All lesser kings are vassals of a wheel-turning monarch, so the wheel-turning monarch is said to be the foremost of them all. …”
